title: Evangelion Movie and Rebuilds
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion Movie and Rebuilds             Quote Post by ROW ROW F.T. POWA » Wed Nov 13, 2024 7:45 pm Hi First time posting on this site, a little strange thing that realized after I watching Evangelion again, I seen twice in my life but felt that seen for a long time even though I it hasn't been a decade since my first viewing, but I always for so unknown reason avoid the Movies but don't know why.

[]: Re: Evangelion Movie and Rebuilds             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Wed Nov 13, 2024 10:20 pm I don't know if this is your case, but when I first watched the original series and EOE, I didn't want to watch the movies afterwards because I didn't want to ruin my perfect experience with the franchise at that moment. It was just too perfect in my mind. But over time I decided to give them a try and it was a good experience. Some of my friends had the exact same experience as me. Maybe that's what's happening to you to some extent.

[]: Re: Evangelion Movie and Rebuilds             Quote Post by ROW ROW F.T. POWA » Thu Nov 14, 2024 6:13 pm That reason sounds likely what I'm experiencing, do you think I should watch it? It's been almost 8 years now..

[]: Re: Evangelion Movie and Rebuilds             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Thu Nov 14, 2024 10:08 pm Yes, I do think so. They're fun for the most part. And they're their own thing. Even if you end up not liking them, that's not going to change what you feel about the original series. Just don't go in expecting them to be like the original series. They're very different in some regards.

[]: Re: Evangelion Movie and Rebuilds             Quote Post by ROW ROW F.T. POWA » Fri Nov 15, 2024 2:20 pm BernardoCairo wrote: Yes, I do think so. They're fun for the most part. And they're their own thing. Even if you end up not liking them, that's not going to change what you feel about the original series. Just don't go in expecting them to be like the original series. They're very different in some regards.
